Name: tensor_core_optimizer
Title: Optimizing Tensor Core Occupancy for Deep Learning Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing tensor core occupancy and memory bandwidth utilization, we can achieve significant speedups in deep learning inference on commodity GPUs without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: While previous work has focused on optimizing CUDA kernel performance, there is a lack of research on optimizing tensor core occupancy and memory bandwidth utilization specifically for deep learning inference on commodity GPUs. Our proposal fills this gap by exploring novel techniques to optimize these critical components.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to optimizing deep learning inference on commodity GPUs by focusing on tensor core occupancy and memory bandwidth utilization. We propose to design and implement a set of optimized CUDA kernels that maximize tensor core occupancy and minimize memory bandwidth usage. Our approach will be evaluated using a range of deep learning models and datasets, with a focus on achieving significant speedups without sacrificing accuracy. We will use profiling tools such as nvprof and Nsight to measure kernel performance and identify bottlenecks.
Experiments: Design and implement optimized CUDA kernels for convolutional and recurrent neural networks
Evaluate the performance of the optimized kernels using a range of deep learning models and datasets
Use profiling tools to measure kernel performance and identify bottlenecks
Compare the performance of the optimized kernels with baseline implementations
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed approach may not generalize to all deep learning models and datasets
The optimized kernels may require significant development and testing time
The performance gains may be limited by other bottlenecks in the system
